chapter_title.ar: باب مَوْلِدِ أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام )
chapter_title.en: Chapter 124 –Arrival of Abu Muhammad Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup> (11<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (158/ 6)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (158/ 6)

parts.text: عَلِيُّ بْنُ زَيْدِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ

text: كَانَ لِي فَرَسٌ وَ كُنْتُ بِهِ مُعْجَباً أُكْثِرُ ذِكْرَهُ فِي الْمَحَالِّ فَدَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ يَوْماً فَقَالَ لِي مَا فَعَلَ فَرَسُكَ فَقُلْتُ هُوَ عِنْدِي وَ هُوَ ذَا هُوَ عَلَى بَابِكَ وَ عَنْهُ نَزَلْتُ فَقَالَ لِيَ اسْتَبْدِلْ بِهِ قَبْلَ الْمَسَاءِ إِنْ قَدَرْتَ عَلَى مُشْتَرِي وَ لَا تُؤَخِّرْ ذَلِكَ وَ دَخَلَ عَلَيْنَا دَاخِلٌ وَ انْقَطَعَ الْكَلَامُ

فَقُمْتُ مُتَفَكِّراً وَ مَضَيْتُ إِلَى مَنْزِلِي فَأَخْبَرْتُ أَخِي الْخَبَرَ فَقَالَ مَا أَدْرِي مَا أَقُولُ فِي هَذَا وَ شَحَحْتُ بِهِ وَ نَفِسْتُ عَلَى النَّاسِ بِبَيْعِهِ وَ أَمْسَيْنَا فَأَتَانَا السَّائِسُ وَ قَدْ صَلَّيْنَا الْعَتَمَةَ فَقَالَ يَا مَوْلَايَ نَفَقَ فَرَسُكَ فَاغْتَمَمْتُ وَ عَلِمْتُ أَنَّهُ عَنَى هَذَا بِذَلِكَ الْقَوْلِ

قَالَ ثُمَّ دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ بَعْدَ أَيَّامٍ وَ أَنَا أَقُولُ فِي نَفْسِي لَيْتَهُ أَخْلَفَ عَلَيَّ دَابَّةً إِذْ كُنْتُ اغْتَمَمْتُ بِقَوْلِهِ فَلَمَّا جَلَسْتُ قَالَ نَعَمْ نُخْلِفُ دَابَّةً عَلَيْكَ يَا غُلَامُ أَعْطِهِ بِرْذَوْنِيَ الْكُمَيْتَ هَذَا خَيْرٌ مِنْ فَرَسِكَ وَ أَوْطَأُ وَ أَطْوَلُ عُمُراً .

en.hubeali: ‘Ali Bin Zayd son of Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> narrated to me saying, ‘There was a horse for me, and I was fascinated by it and would frequently mention it in the gatherings. So I went over to Abu Muhammad<sup>asws</sup> (11<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>) one day, and he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘What happened to your horse?’ So I said, ‘It is with me, and it is that which is at your<sup>asws</sup> door, and I descended from it’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘Change it before the evening if you are able upon (finding a) buyer, and do not delay that’; and a comer entered and the speech was cut off.

So I stood up worried and I went to my house and I informed my brother of the news. So he said, ‘I do not know what I should be saying regarding this’. And I hesitated with it and I was unwilling upon the people with selling it, and evening came. So there came to us a (horse) groomer came over and we prayed the evening Salat, and he said, ‘O my master! Your horse died’. So I was gloomy and knew that this is what heasws had meant with that word.

He (the narrator) said, ‘Then I went over to Abu Muhammad<sup>asws</sup> after a few days, and I was saying within myself, ‘If only he<sup>asws</sup> would replace the animal upon me when I was gloomy due to his<sup>asws</sup> words’. So when I was seated, he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, we<sup>asws</sup> will replace the animal upon you. O boy! Give him my<sup>asws</sup> dark brown horse. This one is better than your horse, and more rideable, and of a longer life-span’.
en.sarwar: 14. Ishaq has said that narrated to him ’Isma‘il ibn Muhammad ibn Ali ibn ’Isma‘il ibn Ali ibn ‘Abdallah ibn ‘Abbass ibn ‘Abd al-Muttalib who has said the following. “Once I sat on the side of the road waiting for abu Muhammad (a.s.) to pass by so that I can present before him my complain against a need. When he was passing by I presented my complain before him and swore that I did not have even a Dirham, nothing for lunch or dinner.” The narrator has said that the Imam (a.s.) said, “You falsely swore by Allah. You have buried two hundred Dinars. These my words are not to deny you any grants. Young man, give him whatever is with you. His servant, give me one hundred Dinars. He then turned to me and said, “You will be deprived of it in your worst days.” He meant those Dinars that I had buried for a difficult day. What he said was true. I had buried two hundred Dinars thinking that they would only be used in a very difficult day. When I faced such a difficult day and all the ways to find a living was closed I went to take out the two hundred Dinars but soon I found that there was not even a single Dinar. In fact, my son had learned about where the Dinars were buried and he had taken them and had ran away. I could not benefit from any of those Dinars.”
